page: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-1200s-amd is an x86_64 architecture server within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz, configured with 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 1200 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. A single complimentary public IPv4 address is provided.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Passmark CPU physics tests also demonstrate strong performance. Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET operations at 42.05 million operations per second, fall within the average range. Large block memory read and write speeds reach 141.36 GB/sec and 71.93 GB/sec respectively.

This hardware profile offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without the premium of dedicated CPU cores, given its shared CPU allocation. It is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-m-24c-192gb-1200s-amd is an x86_64 server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring a 24-core AMD EPYC-Rome processor at 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a 1200 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmarks show strong CPU performance in stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, while memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. This high memory-to-core ratio makes the server qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive tasks that do not require dedicated CPU resources. It is suited for databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy general-purpose workloads.
